findings.description: Overall page layout is not preserved; the HTML reflows the page into a long single-column stream and separates content that is side-by-side in the PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the table appears at top left with the explanatory tax-rate paragraph to its right, followed by a large chart below. In the HTML, the paragraph is moved below the table, content is stacked vertically, and large whitespace appears with chart fragments dispersed down the page.

findings.description: Table structure is degraded and not fully visually intact.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows a compact multi-column table with grouped headers and aligned numeric values. In the HTML, the table is narrower with weakened header grouping, reduced visual alignment, and some columns/labels appear cramped, making the original table structure harder to interpret.

findings.description: Chart/graphic rendering is broken and fragmented in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains one complete, legible combined bar-and-line chart under the table. In the HTML, the chart appears first as a tiny thumbnail, then as separate cropped fragments (including isolated axis/legend/bar portions) placed farther down the page, losing integrity and legibility.

findings.description: Text formatting and hierarchy are only partially preserved.
findings.evidence: The PDF title and figure caption are clearly integrated with the table/chart layout. In the HTML, the title remains, but figure captioning and associated labels are split across the page, and some tabular headers/text lose the strong visual grouping present in the PDF.

findings.description: Reading order differs from the PDF due to moved explanatory text and fragmented chart content.
findings.evidence: A reader of the PDF encounters the table and adjacent explanatory note together, then the full chart. In the HTML, the explanatory note comes after the table, followed by chart thumbnails/fragments and repeated labels farther down, disrupting the original visual sequence.

findings.description: The HTML table omits the dash placeholder in one cell, changing the table content for fiscal year 2007-08 under 'OSBM Civil Penalty & Forfeiture Fund'.
findings.evidence: In the PDF table, row '2007-08' shows a dash '-' under 'OSBM Civil Penalty & Forfeiture Fund'. In the HTML table, the same cell is empty: '' represented effectively by a blank value between collection fees and collection cost columns.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy in HTML does not match the PDF structure. The figure caption and tax-rate sidebar/note are promoted to section headings, which misrepresents document structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF presents 'TABLE 48. FREIGHT CAR LINES TAX COLLECTIONS' as the table title, 'Freight car lines tax rate and base:' as a side note, and 'Figure 48.1 Freight Car Lines Tax Net Collections to General Fund and % Change' as a figure caption. HTML uses '

TABLE 48. FREIGHT CAR LINES TAX COLLECTIONS

' and '

Freight car lines tax rate and base:

', while the figure caption appears as plain paragraph text rather than structured captioning.

findings.description: Chart content is significantly duplicated in HTML: data already present in the table is repeated again as extracted chart text, and the figure caption appears multiple times.
findings.evidence: HTML includes the full table values once in the table and then repeats them in a later '<div>' as lines such as '1996 422,026 -3.15%' through '2010 345,414 88.27%'. The caption 'Figure 48.1 Freight Car Lines Tax Net Collections to General Fund and % Change' also appears more than once.

findings.description: The extracted chart text contains malformed and concatenated text that reduces fidelity and clarity.
findings.evidence: HTML includes garbled text not cleanly reflecting the PDF figure, e.g., 'Fiscal year endCollections to% Change' and '1996 422 026 3 15% $600 100%'. In the PDF, these appear as separate axis labels, legend items, and values within the chart, not as merged phrases.
